zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Smith于大约1410年出生在英国时，他的父亲John Smythe21岁，母亲Joan De Willoughby36岁。他于1465年与Isabell De Toft 14GGM在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Smith was born about 1410, in England, his father, John Smythe, was 21 and his mother, Joan De Willoughby, was 36. He married Isabell De Toft 14GGM in 1465, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Smith est né environ 1410, à Angleterre, son père, John Smythe, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Joan De Willoughby, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Isabell De Toft 14GGM en 1465, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Smith um 1410 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Smythe 21 und seine Mutter Joan De Willoughby 36. Er hat 1465 in Essex, England Isabell De Toft 14GGM geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Smith è nato il circa 1410 nel England, il suo padre, John Smythe, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Joan De Willoughby, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Isabell De Toft 14GGM nel 1465, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Smithが約1410年にイギリスで生まれた時，父John Smytheは21歳，母Joan De Willoughbyは36歳であった。1465年にイギリスエセックスでIsabell De Toft 14GGMと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Smith(이)가 대략 1410년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Smythe(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan De Willoughby(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1465년 영국 에세스에서 Isabell De Toft 14GGM(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Smith nasceu aproximadamente 1410, em Inglaterra, seu pai, John Smythe, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Joan De Willoughby, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabell De Toft 14GGM em 1465, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Smith родился около 1410 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени John Smythe было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Joan De Willoughby, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabell De Toft 14GGM в 1465 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Smith nació aproximadamente 1410, en Inglaterra, su papá, John Smythe, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Joan De Willoughby, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Isabell De Toft 14GGM en 1465, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en su pueblo.
